subject: Computer Networking
title: What Is a Router?
description: A short lesson to help you apply What Is a Router?.

hook.text: Every home has one. It connects you to the internet. But what does a router actually do inside?

buildup.text: A router takes one ISP connection and shares it with all your devices using NAT.

discovery.text: NAT lets dozens of devices share one public IP. The router tracks who asked for what.

twist.text: Your home router is three devices in one: a router, a switch, and a wireless access point.

climax.text: It also runs a DHCP server for local IPs and a firewall to block uninvited traffic.

punchline.text: Your router is a traffic cop, translator, and guard in one box.

quiz.question: What does NAT allow a home router to do?
options: Let many devices share one public IP address
Increase internet speed for all devices
Encrypt all traffic leaving the network
